name: Codex Desktop
notes: OpenAI-signed, hardened, and notarized — but Electron (Chromium 149) with the node-inspect fuse ON.
Verified: relaunching with the direct --remote-debugging-port flag DOES open a CDP endpoint (the earlier CODEX_ELECTRON_CHROMIUM_SWITCHES env-var route is a red herring).
So the same runtime CDP injection works — nothing on disk is copied, patched, or re-signed, and the signature/notarization stay intact.

With generic mode, Codex's real chat messages (markdown

blocks) now render RTL; span-based UI (sidebar titles, some inline) is not yet covered.
Kept experimental and opt-in: it relaunches OpenAI's signed app with a debug port. Not marketed as supported.
